commit.message:
fix: resolve merge conflicts for PokePoke-mdaf

Resolved conflict in tests/test_parallel_support.py by keeping the new
regression test from the feature branch. Removed duplicate guard clause
in dispatch_items to fix complexity and file length violations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: continue dispatch loop past deferred high-conflict items (PokePoke-mdaf)

When a high-conflict item at the front of the queue was deferred by
_should_skip_item(), the made_progress flag stayed False and the outer
while loop broke immediately. This starved normal items behind it because
select_multiple_items(count=1) only returned the top candidate.

Replace the unconditional break with a conditional continue: if new
candidates were evaluated this iteration (attempted_this_cycle grew),
re-invoke select_multiple_items with the deferred IDs in the skip set
so lower-priority non-conflict items can dispatch. Break only when no
new candidates remain.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: filter blocked children during hierarchy resolution

_get_available_children and get_next_child_task in beads_hierarchy.py
did not check has_unmet_blocking_dependencies when selecting child
tasks. This allowed items with unmet blockers to be scheduled when
their parent epic/feature was resolved to a leaf task via
resolve_to_leaf_task, bypassing the blocking dep filter applied at
the top-level in _filter_available and select_multiple_items.

Add has_unmet_blocking_dependencies check to both _get_available_children
and get_next_child_task so blocked children are excluded during hierarchy
traversal.

Fixes PokePoke-bmk7

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: filter blocked children during hierarchy resolution

_get_available_children and get_next_child_task in beads_hierarchy.py
did not check has_unmet_blocking_dependencies when selecting child
tasks. This allowed items with unmet blockers to be scheduled when
their parent epic/feature was resolved to a leaf task via
resolve_to_leaf_task, bypassing the blocking dep filter applied at
the top-level in _filter_available and select_multiple_items.

Add has_unmet_blocking_dependencies check to both _get_available_children
and get_next_child_task so blocked children are excluded during hierarchy
traversal.

Fixes PokePoke-bmk7

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(parallel): enforce solo scheduling for high-conflict items

High-conflict labeled items (high-conflict-risk, merge-conflict-prone)
were being scheduled alongside other work items in parallel mode. This
caused merge conflicts when these items ran concurrently.

Changes to dispatch_items() in parallel_support.py:
- Block new dispatches while a high-conflict item is active
- Defer high-conflict items when other items are already running
- After dispatching a high-conflict item, stop filling remaining slots
- Extract _should_skip_item() helper to reduce cyclomatic complexity

Fixes PokePoke-sz6k

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(parallel): enforce solo scheduling for high-conflict items

High-conflict labeled items (high-conflict-risk, merge-conflict-prone)
were being scheduled alongside other work items in parallel mode. This
caused merge conflicts when these items ran concurrently.

Changes to dispatch_items() in parallel_support.py:
- Block new dispatches while a high-conflict item is active
- Defer high-conflict items when other items are already running
- After dispatching a high-conflict item, stop filling remaining slots
- Extract _should_skip_item() helper to reduce cyclomatic complexity

Fixes PokePoke-sz6k

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: route RunLogger.log_orchestrator() through standard logging

Replace manual file-open-per-write and custom timestamp formatting in
RunLogger with a standard logging.FileHandler on a per-run logger.
Orchestrator events now flow through Python's logging module, enabling
verbosity control, handler attachment, and module-based filtering.

- Add FileHandler to run-scoped logger (pokepoke.orchestrator.run_<id>)
- log_orchestrator() delegates entirely to self._py_logger.log()
- finalize() routes summary lines through log_orchestrator()
- Add close() and context-manager support for handler cleanup
- Update tests to call close() before temp directory cleanup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ui): show cleanup agents with own card type instead of retry label

Cleanup agents (e.g. Merge Conflict Cleanup) with parent_card_id were
incorrectly classified as retry kids and displayed with the
'Retried with feedback' separator. They are a distinct workflow phase.

- Add isCleanupAgent() and getCleanupDisplayLabel() helpers
- Add getCleanupChildren() to agentsPanelHelpers
- Exclude cleanup agents from retry classification in getRetryChildren
- Exclude cleanup agents from attempt label in shouldShowAttemptLabel
- Render cleanup children with dedicated separator and label
- Add CSS for .agent-cleanup-separator styling
- Fix pre-existing test expecting v2 for iteration>1 agent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(desktop): change completed agent dot from green to muted gray

The success indicator dot (.agent-dot-success) used the same green color
(--accent-primary) as the running indicator, making it impossible to
distinguish completed agents from active ones. Changed to use --text-muted
(gray) so only truly running agents show the green pulsing dot.

Fixes: PokePoke-o0dc

commit.message:
fix: preserve worktrees when app exits instead of removing them

When PokePoke shuts down (Ctrl+C, SIGTERM), worktrees were being cleaned
up by cleanup_on_failure() and cleanup_worktree() in the failure path.
This destroyed in-progress work that could otherwise be resumed.

Now check is_shutting_down() before cleanup in both:
- workflow_helpers._finalize_item_result() failure path
- workflow.process_work_item() finally block

Closes PokePoke-0jkl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: update .gitignore for beads runtime files and commit last-touched

Add .beads runtime/daemon files and .pokepoke/sessions to .gitignore
to prevent accidental commits of transient state files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 4 (remove backward-compat re-export shims) requires no changes
because the prerequisite phases (1-3) were never completed. All source
files remain flat in src/pokepoke/ with no subpackage __init__.py shims
to remove.

commit.message:
fix(beads): prevent re-processing of already-closed items

Add is_beads_item_closed() to beads_query.py for live status checks.
Filter closed items in work_item_selection (_filter_available and
select_multiple_items). Check closed status before claiming in
parallel dispatch and add closed items to the skip set.

Three-layer defense:
1. Fast filter on status field from bd ready results
2. Live bd show check before claiming in dispatch_items
3. Closed items added to failed_claim_ids skip set

Fixes PokePoke-v9eq

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(preflight): include error details and rate-limit repeated warnings

The pre-flight health check log message now includes the specific error
details (check_name: message) instead of just a count, so operators can
diagnose failures from the log alone.

Repeated identical failure warnings are rate-limited: logged on the first
occurrence and then every 50th consecutive identical failure, with a note
showing how many occurrences were suppressed. The rate-limit resets when
checks pass or the error signature changes.

The handle_preflight_checks function and rate-limiting helpers are
extracted to preflight_log_utils.py to keep parallel_support.py under
the file length limit.

Closes PokePoke-duy7

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add per-invocation and aggregate timeouts to cleanup agents

Cleanup agents previously inherited the 2-hour DEFAULT_AGENT_TIMEOUT,
wasting 6-12+ hours on unfixable problems across 3 retry attempts.

Changes:
- Add CLEANUP_AGENT_TIMEOUT (10 min) and CLEANUP_AGGREGATE_TIMEOUT (30 min)
- Thread timeout through _run_agent_with_ui to invoke_copilot
- Add aggregate timeout in run_cleanup_loop and check_and_commit_main_repo
- Add recurring error detection to short-circuit on repeated failures
- Extract _run_cleanup_retries helper to reduce function complexity

Closes: PokePoke-lq0i

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: update beads database

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(reconciliation): treat beads_closed as sufficient for reconciliation

reconcile_completed_item() previously required all 3 conditions
(beads_closed AND commits_on_default AND worktree_cleaned), causing
completed items to be re-processed when commits lived on worktree
branches and hadn't been merged yet.

Changes:
- Add worktree_branch_has_commits() helper to check worktree branch
- Change reconciliation logic: beads_closed alone is sufficient
- Add commits_on_worktree_branch to evidence dict
- Update evidence print in workflow_helpers.py

Fixes PokePoke-0tod

commit.message:
fix(beads): move bd sync outside claim lock in assign_and_sync_item

The run_bd_sync_with_retry() call (up to 3x60s=180s) was held inside
the per-item filelock, blocking the agent semaphore slot for the entire
sync duration. Since sync is best-effort visibility and the claim is
already verified before sync runs, move it outside the lock scope so
the agent slot is freed immediately after claim verification succeeds.

This improves effective parallelism by preventing a hung bd sync from
blocking other agents from claiming items.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(merge_queue): set worker thread daemon=True to prevent process hang on ungraceful shutdown

The worker thread was created with daemon=False, which meant if the process
exited ungracefully (unhandled exception, uncaught KeyboardInterrupt), the
non-daemon thread would keep the process alive indefinitely waiting for the
queue to drain. Setting daemon=True allows the process to exit cleanly in
those cases while still relying on the explicit shutdown path
(request_shutdown -> merge_queue.shutdown) for graceful drain.

Cherry-picked from task/PokePoke-jjxz.
Closes: PokePoke-jjxz

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop toAppError regex from masking Prisma errors as 404

The /not found/i regex in toAppError() caught Prisma infrastructure
errors (e.g. 'table not found') and converted them to HTTP 404,
hiding the real error (missing DB, unmigrated tables). This caused
the /api/workspace 404 on Vercel.

- Remove overly broad 'not found' regex from toAppError
- Use explicit AppError(404) in getCurrentWorkspace instead
- Update test mocks to throw AppError matching real service behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: tighten _is_windows_lock_error to match only genuine lock errors

Remove overly broad patterns ('directory not empty', 'device or resource
busy') that could match errors unrelated to Windows file locking.  Add
Windows error code patterns ([WinError 32], [WinError 33]) and the
WinError 33 message text for precise lock detection.

Restructure _handle_worktree_removal_error to always attempt force
removal regardless of error classification, so transient failures like
'directory not empty' caused by a locked file inside still get retried
without being misclassified as a lock error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hooks): use precise test exclusion in coverage check

Replace overly broad 'test not in f' filter with precise exclusions
that correctly handle source files containing 'test' in their name
(e.g. beta_tester.py). The new filter checks for /tests/ directories
and test_ file prefixes, which works with both the current flat layout
and the planned subdirectory restructuring.

Verified all hook glob patterns already use recursive-compatible
matching (startswith 'src/pokepoke/', '^src/pokepoke/') so they
will work correctly when files are moved to domain subpackages.

Closes: PokePoke-41tl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace magic number dialog_type=20 with webview.FOLDER_DIALOG

In browse_for_project(), the dialog_type parameter was hardcoded as 20
with a comment referencing webview.FOLDER_DIALOG. This replaces the
magic number with the actual constant for readability and to avoid
breakage if the value changes in a future pywebview version.

Resolves: PokePoke-5b21

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix flaky pilot detail visual snapshot test (#62)

* Initial plan

* Fix flaky pilot detail visual test: wait for DNA chart and map tiles

The pilot detail page visual snapshot test was flaky because it only
waited for the h1 heading before taking a screenshot, while the page
has dynamically-loaded content (DNA radar chart, Leaflet map) that
takes time to render.

Added two waits before the screenshot:
- Wait for the Pilot DNA section heading (confirms MV data is loaded)
- Wait for map tiles to finish loading (ensures Leaflet map is stable)

Co-authored-by: emilburzo <6228327+emilburzo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert unrelated package-lock.json normalization

Co-authored-by: emilburzo <6228327+emilburzo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wait for DNA chart SVG instead of heading before screenshot

The heading renders immediately when dna data is truthy, but the
PilotDnaChart (loaded via dynamic import with ssr:false) takes
additional time to render its Recharts SVG. Wait for the actual
SVG element inside the DNA section's parent div to confirm the
dynamic import has completed and the chart is fully rendered.

Co-authored-by: emilburzo <6228327+emilburzo@users.noreply.github.com>

* update screenshots

* Revert "update screenshots"

This reverts commit 18acc4a41df19a72189c06e9a3c8654e367e9cba.

feat: Barcode scanner integration for product entry (#10)

Adds camera-based barcode scanning to the "Produkt hinzufügen" form,
automatically resolving scanned codes to product names via the Open Food
Facts API.

## Changes

### `src/barcode.ts` (new)
- `openBarcodeScanner()` — mounts a fullscreen camera modal using
`@zxing/browser`'s `BrowserMultiFormatReader` (supports EAN-8/13, UPC-A,
QR, etc.)
- On scan: queries Open Food Facts (`product_name_de` → `product_name`
fallback) and returns `{ barcode, productName }`
- Falls back to the raw barcode string when API is unreachable or
product is unknown
- Properly releases camera via `IScannerControls.stop()` on close

### `src/app.ts`
- Added **📷 Scannen** button inline with the product name input; on
result, fills the field and focuses it for the user to continue

### `package.json`
- Added `@zxing/browser` + `@zxing/library` as runtime dependencies

## UI

![Barcode scanner button in product
form](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e9c3257d-dfe9-4a9c-9850-e5676f8f813b)
